steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence one states that residents rely heavily on the Metro for millions of trips. Sentence two says city officials maintain it rigorously. The blank must show that the maintenance is a natural consequence of the heavy reliance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For example': signals an illustration. Maintenance is not an example of heavy ridership — it's a response to it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Accordingly': means 'as a result' or 'for that reason.' Officials maintain the system because residents depend on it heavily — direct cause and effect. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Nevertheless': signals a concession or contrast. There is no tension between the two sentences to resolve. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Alternatively': signals a different option or choice. No alternative is being presented here. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
